file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16- AMENDMENT #2 TO THE DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR in [*], subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan for [*] to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4a. For the purposes of this provision, distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers.
perturbation.changed_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR in [*], subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan for [*] to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4a. For the purposes of this provision, distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly or indirectly from NETGEAR, or any affiliated company for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the definition of 'distributor.' The original text specifies that a distributor acquires products *directly* from NETGEAR. The modified text broadens this definition to include companies acquiring products *directly or indirectly* from NETGEAR *or any affiliated company*. This contradicts the exclusivity clause in the same sentence, as NETGEAR could argue that sales through affiliates do not violate the exclusivity agreement with Ingram Micro, since the acquiring entity is not technically acquiring products *directly* from NETGEAR. The exclusivity clause is weakened, creating legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR in [*], subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan for [*] to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4a. For the purposes of this provision, distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly or indirectly from NETGEAR, or any affiliated company for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers. The foregoing notwithstanding, during the [*] and any subsequent period, NETGEAR reserves the right to sell or license Products in [*] to customers other than distributors such as, but not limited to resellers who procure Products at centralized locations for resale to end-use customers solely through their wholly or majority owned retail outlets, both store-front and catalog.
perturbation.justification: The modified text expands the definition of 'distributor' to include entities acquiring products 'directly or indirectly from NETGEAR, or any affiliated company.' This directly contradicts the original exclusivity clause that intended to grant exclusivity to the distributor for products acquired *directly* from NETGEAR. The addition of 'indirectly' and 'affiliated company' allows NETGEAR to potentially circumvent the exclusivity agreement by selling through affiliates, thus selling indirectly, which weakens the exclusivity intended in the contradicted text: 'Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR.' The change creates a strong inconsistency by broadening the definition of Distributor and undermining the exclusivity granted by the contract.

perturbation.original_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede any conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede any conflicting terms of the original Agreement, except for Section 2 of the Original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the terms of the amendment supersede any conflicting terms of the original agreement, *except* for Section 2 of the original agreement. This means that Section 2 of the original agreement remains in effect, even if it conflicts with the Amendment. However Section 1 of this Amendment directly refers to amending Section 2 of the original agreement, creating a clear conflict and ambiguity: Is Section 2 amended or not? The clause introduced here creates uncertainty about which terms should be enforced, especially concerning the territory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms of this Amendment, the Territory listed in Section 2 of the Agreement is amended to be the United States and [*].
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an explicit contradiction. The original text stated that the amendment supersedes any conflicting terms in the original agreement. The changed text creates an exception to this by stating that the amendment supersedes any conflicting terms, except for Section 2. However, the contradicted text (Subject to the terms of this Amendment, the Territory listed in Section 2 of the Agreement is amended...) indicates that Section 2 *is* being amended. Therefore, the changed text creates a clear conflict and ambiguity about whether Section 2 is amended or not, creating an in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
